title: rubus inclinans
intro: Rubus inclinans, also known as the drooping blackberry or creeping blackberry, is a type of bramble that belongs to the rose family. It is a perennial shrub with thorny stems and dark purple berries, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, providing several health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Rubus inclinans can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in jams, jellies, and other food products.
